bikes.blurb: Rad's flagship fat bike, sold in huge numbers from 2021 on. Semi-integrated battery, dual displays, hydraulic brakes. Takeoff supply is deep and parts demand stays steady.
bikes.year_notes: Launched July 2021 (Electrek 2021-07-20) with the dual-screen display, semi-integrated 672Wh pack, internally mounted controller, and hydraulic brakes from day one; no verified mid-production component change through 2024 (checked EBR, forums, launch coverage). The Safe Shield semi-integrated pack (Rad press release 2024-03-05) is a $499 optional upgrade, never standard on this model.

stock_parts.comp_note: No confirmed sold comps; stock Rad saddles ride along in eBay parts lots around $10-20 (searched Jul 2026). Estimated low.

upgrade.name: Cloud-9 Comfort Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Stock seat is a plank; this is the standard first-week fix.

stock_parts.comp_note: Zero standalone resale demand for stock rubber grips; parts-lot filler at best.

upgrade.name: Ergon GP1 lock-on grips
upgrade.note: Locks on and saves your palms on long throttle days.

stock_parts.comp_note: Generic Wellgo takeoffs, filler in parts lots; no standalone sold comps found (Jul 2026). Estimated at $10.

upgrade.name: Race Face Chester composite pedals
upgrade.note: Bigger platform and real pins; stock pedals get slick when wet.

stock_parts.comp_note: New pair runs $110-250 retail (Kenda, Rad, eBay listings Jul 2026); active used listings seen but zero confirmed sold takeoff pairs. Estimated $50/pair. Same tire spec across the full 2021-2024 run.

upgrade.name: Origin8 Supercell 26x4 street tires (pair)
upgrade.note: Street slick pair; kills the knobby hum and rolls faster on pavement.

stock_parts.stock_part: Rad dual-screen set: LED remote + center LCD
stock_parts.catalog_name: Rad dual-screen display set (LED remote + LCD)
stock_parts.comp_note: New Rad UI display sells for $110 at dealers (Jul 2026); no direct sold comps for the dual set. One forum-cited combo sale ($150 for controller + LCD, Feb 2022) anchors the ceiling. Estimated $45. Dual-screen setup verified unchanged across the 2021-2024 run (launch coverage + EBR reviews) — no year split.

upgrade.name: EggRider V2 Bluetooth Display
upgrade.note: Bluetooth tuning and real ride data; unlocks settings Rad hides.

stock_parts.comp_note: Forum-cited $150 eBay sale for a controller + LCD combo (Feb 2022); new Rad controller $110 at dealers (Jul 2026). Alone estimated $40. Internal-mount controller from launch; no mid-run controller change found (the Canadian 500W-limited controller is a regional variant, not a year change — ebikesforum.com thread).

upgrade.name: Bolton Ebikes 35A controller + color LCD kit
upgrade.note: Doubles usable power from the stock motor; the classic Rad hot-rod move.

stock_parts.comp_note: 3 eBay sellers with 29, 25, and 11 recorded sales (Jul 2026); new OEM $395, used/refurb marketed near 50% off. Semi-integrated packs track the external market. $200 is conservative for a tested pack. Same pack all years; some trade-ins may arrive with the 2024+ Safe Shield semi-integrated upgrade pack ($499 new, UL 2271, requires updated charger) — worth more, grade it separately if seen.
Multiple pre-owned OEM Rad fork listings at $129.99 on eBay (Jul 2026); asking prices, not solds. Estimated $80.

upgrade.name: RST Renegade fat bike air fork
upgrade.note: Air spring with damping; the stock coil fork is a pogo stick.

bikes.blurb: Commuter workhorse with the RadRover 6 Plus electronics in a 27.5 package. Owners swap contact points fast and the battery holds value.
bikes.year_notes: Launched September 2021 (Electrek 2021-09-22) with dual displays, hydraulic discs, and the semi-integrated pack from day one; still sold through 2024-2025 (Electric Bike Report 2024 review) with no documented component changes. Safe Shield semi-integrated pack is a 2024+ optional upgrade only.

bikes.blurb: The budget moped-style runabout. Single speed, LED control pad, external battery. Cheap bike, so takeoffs are cheap, except the battery.
bikes.year_notes: Released late 2021 as a MY2022 bike (Electrek review 2022-02-11); LED control pad, single-speed drivetrain, mechanical discs, and external pack stayed identical through the run; discontinued by late 2025 (bikeride.com community note, Oct 2025). No year variants found.

bikes.blurb: 2023 refresh of the RadRunner with hydraulic brakes, an RST suspension fork, 7-speed drivetrain and a semi-integrated battery. Newer electronics mean a thin used market for now.
bikes.year_notes: Launched March 2023 (Electrek 2023-03-01) and unchanged since; EBR's spec sheet confirms the SEMI-INTEGRATED 672Wh pack (v1 wrongly implied the external pack), dual grayscale screens (2.5in center LCD + 1.5in remote), Tektro HD-E350 hydraulics, and RST 60mm spring fork from launch. Safe Shield semi-integrated pack is a 2024+ optional upgrade, not standard.

stock_parts.stock_part: Rad dual-screen set: 2.5in grayscale center LCD + 1.5in LCD remote
stock_parts.catalog_name: Rad dual-screen display set (RadRunner 3, LCD remote)
stock_parts.comp_note: Renamed from v1's 'Rad UI display': EBR's spec sheet shows this is the dual grayscale screen setup (2.5in center + 1.5in secondary LCD), not the 2024+ Rad UI color unit. New replacement displays run ~$110 at dealers (Jul 2026); no used solds found. Estimated $45.

New RadRunner 3 wiring and controllers stocked at dealers ($49-110, Jul 2026); no used solds found. Aftermarket kits do not target this platform yet. Estimated $40. No mid-run controller change documented (2023-2026).

Corrected from v1: EBR spec sheet and Rad's own Safe Shield compatibility page confirm the RadRunner 3 Plus uses the SAME semi-integrated pack as RadRover 6 Plus / RadCity 5 Plus, so it inherits that pack's solid comps: 3 eBay sellers with 29, 25, and 11 recorded sales (Jul 2026); new OEM $395, used near 50% off. 2024+ Safe Shield semi-integrated ($499) is an optional upgrade pack.

EBR spec sheet: RST spring suspension, 60mm travel, preload + lockout. OEM Rad forks list used around $129.99 on eBay (Jul 2026); no direct comps for this unit. Estimated $70.

bikes.blurb: Gen 1 RadRunner with the works: LCD, suspension fork, fenders, passenger-ready. Discontinued, so its parts feed the big RadRunner fleet still on the road.
bikes.year_notes: Announced March 2020 with first deliveries July 2020 (Electrek 2020-03-26); SW-LCD display with USB, RST 80mm spring fork, and 7-speed Acera drivetrain were standard for the entire run, which ended when the RadRunner 3 Plus arrived in early 2023. No year variants found.

bikes.blurb: The 22-inch-wheel cargo hauler with the infamous tire recall. Check the sidewall before crediting any tire. Everything else trades like standard Rad kit.
bikes.year_notes: In production March 2020 through 2023. CPSC recall announced Nov 3, 2022 (137 tire blowout/separation reports, 8 injuries) covers the original ribbed-sidewall Vee 22x3 tires; the remedy replaced both tires AND rim strips with a redesigned smooth-sidewall version, so any 2020-2022 bike may carry either tire — identify by sidewall, not year. No other component changed mid-run.

stock_parts.stock_part: Vee Tire 22x3 custom tires, ribbed sidewall (recalled original)
stock_parts.comp_note: CPSC recall Nov 3, 2022 after 137 blowout and separation reports and 8 injuries (cpsc.gov recall alert). The recalled original has a RIBBED sidewall. Do not buy, credit, or resell these. No legitimate market.

Proprietary 22x3 size only Rad supports keeps spares demand alive; no used solds found (Jul 2026). Estimated $40/pair.

bikes.blurb: 2024 cargo redo: torque sensor, 720Wh Safe Shield pack, hydraulic brakes, standard 20-inch wheels. Too new for a real takeoff market yet.
bikes.year_notes: Announced March 5, 2024 (Rad press release; one of four Safe Shield launch models) with the torque sensor, 90Nm motor, Tektro hydraulics, full-color USB-C display, and UL 2271 Safe Shield 720Wh external pack standard from day one; no changes documented since. Tires corrected to Kenda 20x3.3 (GearJunkie/EBR reviews), not 20x3.0.

Likely crosses over with the RadRunner K-Rad pattern but tread not confirmed, so kept as its own row. Bike launched 2024; takeoffs scarce, no used solds. Estimated $40/pair.

stock_parts.stock_part: Rad UI full-color display w/ USB-C + backlit remote
stock_parts.comp_note: Year-qualified rename from v1's generic 'Rad UI display': 2024+ models (RadWagon 5, Radster, RadExpand 5 Plus) use the full-COLOR display with USB-C (EBReport review), distinct from the grayscale dual-screen on 2021-2023 platforms. New ~$110 at dealers (Jul 2026); used market thin, no solds found. Estimated $45.

Standard from launch (UL 2271, potted cells; Rad press 2024-03-05). Safe Shield packs run $479-599 new at dealers (Jul 2026); no used solds found yet. UL-certified pack should hold value but evidence is thin. Estimated $220. Requires the updated Rad charger.

Split from v1's shared 'Rad suspension fork 20in (2023+ models)' name: the RadWagon 5 fork is a bolt-on thru-axle cargo unit, not the QR RST fork on the RadRunner 3 Plus. OEM Rad forks list used around $129.99 on eBay (Jul 2026); asking not sold. Estimated $70.
Owner-group asking prices $30-50 for deck hardware; no solds confirmed (Jul 2026). Estimated $30.

bikes.blurb: Folding 20x4 runabout on the classic Rad external-battery platform. LED control pad, mechanical discs, rigid fork. Same electronics as the old fleet, so parts cross over.
bikes.year_notes: Launched April 2022 (Electrek 2022-04-29) replacing the RadMini. EBR confirms it shipped with the LED control pad — the monochrome LCD was a $99 UPGRADE, not standard (v1 wrongly listed the SW-LCD as stock) — plus 180mm mechanical discs and the external 672Wh pack, all unchanged through the run. It stayed on sale alongside the separate hydraulic-brake RadExpand 5 Plus after March 2024; exact end-of-run not firmly documented.

bikes.blurb: Discontinued single-speed city bike on a lighter 500W platform. Batteries are the only takeoff with real legs; owners hunt spares.
bikes.year_notes: Single production run: launched late 2020 as a MY2020 bike at $999-1,199 (Electrek/Engadget Nov 2020 reviews) and dropped in Rad's 2022 lineup cut. LED control pad (no LCD), 500W motor, slim 10.5Ah pack, and Tektro Aries mechanicals throughout — no year variants exist.

bikes.blurb: The classic external-battery fat bike that built Rad. Biggest mod community of the bunch; Bolton kits, tire swaps and hydraulic conversions are routine.
bikes.year_notes: Sold 2019-2021 (EBR 2019 review; 2021 units documented on Upway) until the RadRover 6 Plus took over (announced July 2021). King-Meter SW-LCD, 48V 14Ah Samsung external pack, external controller, Tektro Aries mechanicals, and RST spring fork ran the whole production — the internal-controller and hydraulic-brake changes belong to the RadRover 6 Plus, not any RadRover 5 model year.
